subject: DevOps
title: Audit Logging
description: A fast breakdown of Audit Logging for builders.

hook.text: Who deleted the database? When? From where? Without audit logs, those questions have no answers.

buildup.text: Audit logs record who did what, when, and where. Essential for investigations and compliance.

discovery.text: CloudTrail, GCP Audit Logs, and Azure Activity Logs capture every API call. Every action recorded.

twist.text: Logs must be tamper-proof. Use write-once storage. If attackers edit logs, the trail is worthless.

climax.text: Centralize logs from all services. Correlate events across systems. One timeline reveals the story.

punchline.text: Log everything, protect the logs, centralize them. Logs tell the story.

quiz.question: Why must audit logs be stored in tamper-proof storage?
options: So attackers can't delete or modify evidence of their actions
Audit logs take up too much regular storage space
